program_en: Interdisciplinary Major Programme in Global Economics and Finance
program_chi: 環球經濟與金融跨學科主修課程
learning_outcomes:
GLEFN_Learning Outcomes_ENG 1. Major
Programme: Upon completion of
the Programme, students will have:
Knowledge outcomes
(K) acquired
an understanding of the complex interrelationships among the world
economies in terms of the flow of goods, services, and funds. (K1) studied
the functions and operations of financial markets and institutions, and
the interaction among them in a globalised context. (K2) analysed
the wide range of financial instruments in the globalised economies with
regard to their design, properties, and pricing. (K3) Skills outcomes (S) equipped
students with the ability to use econometric and mathematical modeling tools
in order to: (a) understand and
analyse emerging trends in global financial markets and institutions,
(b) support financial decision making at a corporate or individual level, and
(c) formulate global strategies for business and public organisations in an
increasingly complex financial landscape. (S1) developed
generic skills of communication in verbal and written form and of
collaboration with peers in a team work environment. (S2) Values outcomes
(V)
Nurtured virtues that are conducive to good personal ethics, such as: Honesty
and integrity (V1) Cross-cultural
sensitivity and tolerance (V2) Capacity
of life-long learning. (V3)

Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  GLEF
courses at 2000 and above level as well as those labeled with # will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  GLEF
majors are not allowed to declare minor in Economics.
3.  |  Double
majors in GLEF and ECON, GLEF and IFAA, and GLEF and QFIN are not
allowed.
4.  |  Students
of GLEF Programme who wish to claim IBBA as second major must choose a
concentration area other than the Finance concentration area.
[a]  |  Students
are advised to take MGNT2512 once their Global Experiential Learning
Activities are completed.
[b]  |  Subject
to the approval of the major programme, students who have completed the
Co-operative Education Programme are deemed to have fulfilled the
capstone requirement GLEF4070.
[ ]  |  Subject
area code “DSME” changed to “DOTE” with effect from 2024-25.
